result.summary: Back4app exposes a Parse REST API at `https://parseapi.back4app.com` authenticated with `X-Parse-Application-Id` + `X-Parse-REST-API-Key`, a Parse GraphQL API at `https://parseapi.back4app.com/graphql` authenticated with `X-Parse-Application-Id` + `X-Parse-Client-Key` (optionally plus `X-Parse-Session-Token` for logged-in user context), and a stdio MCP server run via `npx @back4app/mcp-server-back4app` authenticated with a Back4app account key/personal access token.

app_id.label: Back4app Application ID
app_id.setup: Create a Back4app app, then in your app dashboard open **App Settings → Security & Keys** to copy the `Application ID`. The quickstart says you always need the `Application ID` plus another key appropriate to the SDK/API you use. Start from the [Quickstart](https://www.back4app.com/docs/get-started/welcome).

account_key.label: Back4app account key / personal access token
account_key.setup: Log into Back4app, open the **Hello, [username]** menu, go to **Account Keys**, create a new key, and copy it securely; the docs note you won't be able to see it again. This token is used by Back4app MCP and the Back4app CLI. See [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://www.back4app.com/docs/mcp) and [Command Line Interface Setup](https://www.back4app.com/docs/local-development/parse-cli).

session_token.label: Parse user session token
session_token.setup: This is an end-user session token obtained by logging a user in or signing a user up through the Parse API, then sending it in `X-Parse-Session-Token` for user-context operations. This is documented for GraphQL, but it is an application user's credential rather than a developer-issued integration credential. See [Authenticating a logged user through the Parse GraphQL API](https://www.back4app.com/docs/parse-graphql/graphql-user-authentication).
